nativeRange.habitat: Found on reef crests and in lagoons, typically at depths of 6–65 m, most commonly between 6–20 m near coral heads.

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, and quality prepared carnivore foods. Tends to dart out from cover to snag passing prey items. A varied diet has been noted to help maintain coloration.

summary: The Ring Eyed Dottyback is a small, secretive reef fish from the Western Pacific, generally considered reef-safe and well suited to nano and mid-sized aquariums. Males tend to display pinkish-red coloration while females are paler with yellow-green gradations on the fins.
careNotes: The Ring Eyed Dottyback (Pseudoplesiops typus) is a notoriously secretive species that typically retreats into rockwork and crevices, occasionally dashing out to snag passing food. It is generally considered reef-safe and has been reported to accept a variety of frozen and prepared foods in captivity. It is best kept singly; individuals have been known to harass smaller, passive tank mates such as nano gobies, particularly in confined spaces. The species displays sexual dichromatism — a difference in coloration between sexes — with males tending toward pinkish-red and females being paler pink with yellow-green gradations on the fins. A diet that includes vitamin-enriched foods has been associated with better color retention.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (4.6 cm SL), temperature range (26.6–29°C), native range, and taxonomy. Temperature disagreement with LiveAquaria noted — ranges do not overlap; averaged values used and dataStatus set to needs_review.
